title: haronga crenata
side_effects: Haronga crenata can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Prolonged consumption of the plant may lead to gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea, nausea, and stomach pain. Improper use of Haronga crenata can also cause dizziness, headaches, and fatigue in some individuals. In severe cases, excessive consumption can lead to cardiovascular complications and interact negatively with certain medications.
